title: Austrobassia longicuspis
intro: Austrobassia longicuspis, also known as sandalwood, is a type of evergreen tree native to Australia, valued for its distinctive, woody fragrance and medicinal properties. The essential oil extracted from Austrobassia longicuspis has been traditionally used to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Austrobassia longicuspis include sesquiterpenes, specifically α-santalol and β-santalol, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Austrobassia longicuspis is commonly used in aromatherapy, herbal teas, and skincare products to harness its health benefits and fragrance.
taxonomy: Austrobassia longicuspis is classified within the family Myoporaceae, which is a group of flowering plants native to Australia. This species belongs to the genus Austrobassia, which is a monotypic genus, meaning it consists of a single species. The classification of Austrobassia longicuspis is also understood to be part of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The family Myoporaceae is sometimes placed within the order Lamiales, but this can vary depending on the source.
